HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion for Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication In The Alternative.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted.

Plaintiff claims that she was not properly served with defendant's motion and she learned of it while examining the court's register of actions. Defendant's proof of service indicates timely service. Plaintiff opposes the motion on the merits and waived any alleged defect in service. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. (Citations.) This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."].)

Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint alleges that the doors of the bus pinned her "hand, leg, foot, and purse" for "a substantial amount of time" and she incurred injury. (Second Amended Complaint, par. 10; see also par. 13.)

The undisputed evidence demonstrates that only plaintiff's purse was caught in the door and defendant did not injure plaintiff as a matter of law.

"[W]here the facts are such that the only reasonable conclusion is an absence of causation, the question is one of law, not fact." (Modisette v. Apple, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 136, 152.) "When opposing parties tell two different stories, one of which is blatantly contradicted by the record, so that no reasonable jury could believe it, a court should not adopt that version of facts for purpose of ruling on a summary judgment." (Scott v. Harris (2007) 550 U.S. 372, 380 [approving summary judgment for defendant on video evidence over plaintiff's inconsistent testimony]; see also Swigart v. Bruno (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 529, 534 fn. 4 ["To the extent [plaintiff's] witnesses' testimony was inconsistent with the video, we do not consider such inconsistency a disputed fact and have relied on the evidence in the video"].)

Here, defendant's video and other evidence show that the bus door only pinned plaintiff's purse-not any portion of her body. (Shaikh Decl., Ex. 1; Sherron Decl., pars. 8-12.) Plaintiff therefore fails to demonstrate that defendant caused plaintiff cognizable damage. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact in her opposition.

On August 17, 2022, the court granted defendant's unopposed discovery sanctions motion. (Order filed October 24, 2022.) That motion was granted in its entirety and sought evidentiary sanctions. As a result of that order, plaintiff is precluded from presenting evidence "consisting of the 'What, Where, Who, When, Why and How' of how this event occurred [and] evidence or testimony on the injuries she allegedly sustained." (Defendant's Points and Authorities in support of the motion to compel further discovery and for sanctions filed July 22, 2022.) Plaintiff therefore cannot introduce evidence to create a triable issue of material fact.

Lastly, the court notes that plaintiff's opposing separate statement does not comply with CCP 437c(b)(3) as plaintiff disputes numerous facts but does not cite any opposing evidence to create an actual dispute. That dereliction alone is grounds to grant this motion. (See CCP 437c(b)(3) ["Failure to comply with this requirement of a separate statement may constitute a sufficient ground, in the court's discretion, for granting the motion."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/VP)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Patrina Harrison's motion to strike Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Answer to the Amended Complaint is denied.

In this case, Plaintiff allegedly sustained injuries on a Muni vehicle operated by Defendant. Plaintiff moves to strike the affirmative defenses in Defendant's answer, which Defendant opposes. As stated in this Court's previous ruling on demurrer, Defendant need not allege facts to support any affirmative defenses that negate elements of Plaintiff's claims, as Plaintiff will bear the burden of proof on those matters at trial. Moreover, California Code of Civil Procedure section 431.30(g) requires only that "the defenses shall be separately stated, and the several defenses shall refer to the causes of action which they are intended to answer, in a manner by which they may be intelligibly distinguished." Therefore, the Court finds Defendant's affirmative defenses meet the pleading standard.

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/SRB)

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Patrina Harrison's demurrer to Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Answer to the Amended Complaint is overruled without leave to amend.

In this case, Plaintiff allegedly sustained injuries on a Muni vehicle operated by Defendant. Plaintiff demurs to the affirmative defenses in Defendant's answer, which Defendant opposes. Defendant need not allege facts to support any affirmative defenses that negate elements of Plaintiff's claims, as Plaintiff will bear the burden of proof on those matters at trial. Moreover, California Code of Civil Procedure section 431.30(g) requires only that "the defenses shall be separately stated, and the several defenses shall refer to the causes of action which they are intended to answer, in a manner by which they may be intelligibly distinguished." For this reason, the Court overrules Plaintiff's demurrer.

Order is signed.
